item.title: Revisions
item.content:encoded:
After nearly a year, i've returned with some changes.

  • Removed final log between Ethics Committee and Dr. Calloway, I was never that convinced by it.
  • Made edits towards both grammar and tone within MTF and addenda notes.
  • Made edits towards test logs, (4) actual logs remain.
  • Made edits to early description, edited/moved sections to footnotes.
  • Made LOTS of edits in regards to suggestions by gee0765, thanks so much!
  • Adjustments and acknowledgements towards the final discovery log, Changed the story slightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In documenting an SCP, normally the description would describe why something is anomalous. The description for this one doesn't do that.

Maybe some addendum does, but I never got there because "bees that are slightly more colorful and reproduce slightly faster" was not interesting, so I stopped reading.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Okay, let's do this.

+ Nitpicks
– hide block

I'm not gonna go over everything here, because quite a lot stands out as incorrect. I will point out the worst offenders.

In the conprocs, the term "SCP-4276" is overused. I would definitely change this. Terms like 'instances' can help, as can rewording or cutting some sentences.

Class-3 personnel or higher, moreover, it must first be approved by Class-4-4276

Level-3 and Level-4. I also think that each of these levels are one level too high. Level-4 is Site Director level.

more commonly known as the cuckoo wasp

This should be a footnote. The 'cuckoo wasp' section should be plural (wasps) as you're talking about the whole family.

SCP-4276 shares many similarities to its relatives

This phrasing is weird. It should be "with its relatives", and I'm not sure about 'shares many similarities'.

SCP-4276, however, appears to possess a greater diversity

I'd recommend swapping SCP-4276 and however. It flows better, and it would stop this being the third sentence in a row that begins with SCP-4276.

and combinations of the group

This whole phrase can be cut.

1.2 cm (0.5 inches) to upwards of 5.1 cm (2.0 inches)

This doesn't need inches at all. Also, I don't get why the footnote is there. Just replace the larger number with the size of the largest instance.

Lambda-1: "Search the building. Remember protocol."

You don't need quotation marks in a Log.

The pup appears relatively sound save for a cavity in its head.

'Pup' is not clinical.

his innards [REDACTED]

There is no need for this redaction. It's unnecessary and could be filled in with actual words.

SCP-4276's primary anomalous properties

This whole quoteblock doesn't need to be in a quoteblock. Also, every single blackbox is unnecessary.

I'm gonna stop analysing individual lines now, as these problems should have been fixed before this was posted.

– hide block
+ Overall
– hide block

Overall, I'm not really a fan. The clinical tone isn't great, and the dialogue feels very unrealistic in several sections. It doesn't read like things that actual people would say, and the doctors break character far too much. The use of redactions, expungements and blackboxes is far too common, and I feel like literally none of them are justified.

This SCP definitely overstays its welcome. I was struggling to stay focused after the first third, and the final plot twist doesn't work for me. I see no reason why an ethics committee member would be behind this. And there is nothing that hints at it before. All of the MTF logs etc. don't really get anywhere, and the attempts at body horror in them don't really work for me. They're almost too detached and clinical for them to have any effect, and they seem entirely unnecessary having finished the whole SCP.

Overall, I feel like this attempts to do far too many different things, and when combined with a sub-par execution this has led me to a pretty strong downvote.

– hide block

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They learned new things, but his blatant disregard may have put other personnel in danger. Hope you change your mind!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and then immediately demoted to D-class for negligence.

And that's a downvote for me. No one knew about the auditory stimulation, and the whole "demoted to d-class" shit is ridiculously over the top for a first-time offense.

item.title: Re: Great reading but small change
Great reading but small change
item.content:encoded: I suggest you add all instances at the beginning

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see, now it makes sense.

I'll change it to no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed it up a little to help it out for you, I had the idea in my mind but I guess it never put it onto paper. I hope you still enjoyed the article!

item.title: Re: Good, but some issues
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much! Yeah, i'm currently in the process of cleaning up all my logs, and I really want to fit in the final log, but I just can't seem to find an appropriate place to put it. I may very well remove it and use that for another article in the future.

The language is in editing as we speak, so thanks alot!

item.title: Good, but some issues
item.content:encoded: I really liked it, and it was good for the most part, so +1. It does l, however, suffer from some clinical tone issues. The dialogue in the mtf logs seems almost… Juvenile, in places. I also think, and this is just my opinion, that the final log is unnecessary. The stinger in the final MTF log is enough, IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there, thanks for the response. It appears that my wording may have given you a different interpretation than I originally entailed. let me explain.

Johanson didn't cause the High School Incident, rather, he was the one who authorized the slaughter of all of the infected. Evidence supports the idea that the wasps can be treated in hosts, which in turn means that people can be saved. Calloway is frustrated that Johanson would rather eradicate life involved with the skip than try to save it.

I'm definitely going over it again, and I'm sorry for any confusion I've caused. I hope you one day change your stance on your vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not entirely clear on why the foundation decided to investigate a random missing persons' case. I might be missing something, but there's nothing that came up before their investigation that suggested anything anomalous was going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article clearly has some work put in it, but I fell like it really needs some more.

It started to feel wierd in the beginning with "250.0 g mix". You don't really need the .0 part unless 250.9 g mix gives vastly different result.

But it went to a very strange direction, in my opinion:

+ show block
– hide block

While I feel that "The Mother Nature is rising against us" idea is a little bit overused, it is fine and it is executed with a touch of bodily horror.

But the revelation in the end that an Ethics Comittee member is behind an outbreak of an SCP feels very wierd. I rememer a similar idea used for the Shy Guy (096), but I feel it was better there. Let me elaborate:

A researcher, staging a containment breach to prove the dangers of a scip to O5-council - that is compelling, it serves a narrative purpose and explores something about the Foundation and the idea of a termination of and SCP.

In this article an EC-member stages a containment breach - but for what? The SCP was contained, it doesn't really require termination so what was the point? To show the reader that Dr. Calloway is a compulsive, volatile individual? To show that EC is corruptable or maybe even inhuman? While the latter is an interesting idea to explore, it gets really mudded by the added touch of rebelling nature. And the resulting dialog feels inconsistent and misplaced

I feel sorry for it, but as it stands, I feel compeled to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the response! Yeah I can definitely see what you're talking about, and I'll be sure to go over it with another fine cone over the next couple days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good concept, but this article needs some more work.

Crit here
Crit here

It's clear you've put in a lot of work on this, but a lot of the grammar and syntax choices are very difficult to overlook. Use of certain words like "striking" and "absolutely" make the article feel less clinical than it should be. Dr.Calloway instead of Dr. Calloway, overuse of the word "instances", etc. The article would have benefited from another 2 or 3 passes for revision.

"Cuckoo wasps are related to over species of 3000 bees, wasps, and other insects, primarily through their physique and personality. One of the most striking physical features of the species is their shiny metallic carapaces, which have been designed with microscopic structures so thin they interfere with visible light." Explaining the non-anomalous species outside of footnotes, or in direct contrast to the scip, feels out of place, even as a run-up to it's differences.

The article tends to be a bit redundant. A lot of the narrative you're trying to show the reader could be condensed. Especially the test logs, and double MTF logs.

All that said, you're on the verge of a really good article. MTF logs are strong, but the dialogue tone tends to jump around a bit. The closing addendum is equally provocative, but the escalation and contrast feel unbelievable. Dr. Calloway comes in like a bat out of hell, but Johanson remains cool and collected the entire time.

Overall, in it's current state, it's a no vote for me. Keep at it though, there's a lot of potential here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good job, buddy. +1 from me, no surprise.

I don't have anything further to add beyond the previous critique I provided, looks great. I'll reiterate I very much like your field agent dialog as I feel it reads naturally for their type of work, but maybe I've just got a dirty mouth :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Y'all! This is my first ever Skip on the website, so I hope you enjoy it!

Thanks so much to AKFrost, Mew-ltiverse, and wam3483 for their criticism and feedback! Really appreciate it guys!

Image source is Wikimedia Commons: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Chrysididae#/media/File:Cuckoo_wasp_06208.JPG
